root_text.an11.2:1.3: Dhammatā esā, bhikkhave, yaṁ sīlavato sīlasampannassa avippaṭisāro uppajjati.
root_text.an11.2:2.3: Dhammatā esā, bhikkhave, yaṁ avippaṭisārissa pāmojjaṁ uppajjati.
root_text.an11.2:3.3: Dhammatā esā, bhikkhave, yaṁ pamuditassa pīti uppajjati.
root_text.an11.2:4.3: Dhammatā esā, bhikkhave, yaṁ pītimanassa kāyo passambhati.
root_text.an11.2:5.3: Dhammatā esā, bhikkhave, yaṁ passaddhakāyo sukhaṁ vediyati.
root_text.an11.2:6.3: Dhammatā esā, bhikkhave, yaṁ sukhino cittaṁ samādhiyati.
root_text.an11.2:7.3: Dhammatā esā, bhikkhave, yaṁ samāhito yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati.
root_text.an11.2:8.1: Yathābhūtaṁ, bhikkhave, jānato passato na cetanāya karaṇīyaṁ:
root_text.an11.2:8.3: Dhammatā esā, bhikkhave, yaṁ yathābhūtaṁ jānaṁ passaṁ nibbindati.
root_text.an11.2:9.3: Dhammatā esā, bhikkhave, yaṁ nibbinno virajjati.
root_text.an11.2:10.3: Dhammatā esā, bhikkhave, yaṁ viratto vimuttiñāṇadassanaṁ sacchikaroti.
root_text.an11.2:11.1: Iti kho, bhikkhave, virāgo vimuttiñāṇadassanattho vimuttiñāṇadassanānisaṁso, nibbidā virāgatthā virāgānisaṁsā, yathābhūtañāṇadassanaṁ nibbidatthaṁ nibbidānisaṁsaṁ, samādhi yathābhūtañāṇadassanattho yathābhūtañāṇadassanānisaṁso, sukhaṁ samādhatthaṁ samādhānisaṁsaṁ, passaddhi sukhatthā sukhānisaṁsā, pīti passaddhatthā passaddhānisaṁsā, pāmojjaṁ pītatthaṁ pītānisaṁsaṁ, avippaṭisāro pāmojjattho pāmojjānisaṁso, kusalāni sīlāni avippaṭisāratthāni avippaṭisārānisaṁsāni.
root_text.an11.2:11.2: Iti kho, bhikkhave, dhammā dhamme abhisandenti, dhammā dhamme paripūrenti apārā pāraṁ gamanāyā”ti.

translation_text.an11.2:1.1: “Mendicants, an ethical person, who has fulfilled ethical conduct, need not make a wish:
translation_text.an11.2:1.3: It’s only natural that an ethical person has no regrets.
translation_text.an11.2:2.1: When you have no regrets you need not make a wish:
translation_text.an11.2:2.3: It’s only natural that joy springs up when you have no regrets.
translation_text.an11.2:3.1: When you feel joy you need not make a wish:
translation_text.an11.2:3.3: It’s only natural that rapture arises when you’re joyful.
translation_text.an11.2:4.1: When your mind is full of rapture you need not make a wish:
translation_text.an11.2:4.3: It’s only natural that your body becomes tranquil when your mind is full of rapture.
translation_text.an11.2:5.1: When your body is tranquil you need not make a wish:
translation_text.an11.2:5.3: It’s only natural to feel bliss when your body is tranquil.
translation_text.an11.2:6.1: When you feel bliss you need not make a wish:
translation_text.an11.2:6.2: ‘May my mind be immersed in samādhi!’
translation_text.an11.2:6.3: It’s only natural for the mind to become immersed in samādhi when you feel bliss.
translation_text.an11.2:7.1: When your mind is immersed in samādhi you need not make a wish:
translation_text.an11.2:7.3: It’s only natural to truly know and see when your mind is immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.an11.2:8.1: When you truly know and see you need not make a wish:
translation_text.an11.2:8.3: It’s only natural to grow disillusioned when you truly know and see.
translation_text.an11.2:9.1: When you’re disillusioned you need not make a wish:
translation_text.an11.2:9.3: It’s only natural to grow dispassionate when you’re disillusioned.
translation_text.an11.2:10.1: When you’re dispassionate you need not make a wish:
translation_text.an11.2:10.2: ‘May I realize the knowledge and vision of freedom!’
translation_text.an11.2:10.3: It’s only natural to realize the knowledge and vision of freedom when you’re dispassionate.
translation_text.an11.2:11.1: And so, mendicants, the knowledge and vision of freedom is the purpose and benefit of dispassion. Dispassion is the purpose and benefit of disillusionment. Disillusionment is the purpose and benefit of truly knowing and seeing. Truly knowing and seeing is the purpose and benefit of immersion. Immersion is the purpose and benefit of bliss. Bliss is the purpose and benefit of tranquility. Tranquility is the purpose and benefit of rapture. Rapture is the purpose and benefit of joy. Joy is the purpose and benefit of not having regrets. Not having regrets is the purpose and benefit of skillful ethics.
translation_text.an11.2:11.2: And so, mendicants, good qualities flow on and fill up from one to the other, for going from the near shore to the far shore.”
